title: 17.The Longest Suspension Bridge in the World
filename: 17.The Longest Suspension Bridge in the World

lines.english: Lesson 17
lines.chinese: 第17课
lines.raw: Lesson 17 | 第17课

lines.english: The longest suspension bridge in the world
lines.chinese: 世界上最长的吊桥
lines.raw: The longest suspension bridge in the world | 世界上最长的吊桥

lines.english: How is the bridge supported?
lines.chinese: 这座桥是如何支撑起来的？
lines.raw: How is the bridge supported? | 这座桥是如何支撑起来的？

lines.english: Verrazano, an Italian about whom little is known, sailed into New York Harbour in 1524 and named it Angouleme.
lines.chinese: 韦拉扎诺，一位鲜为人知的意大利人，于1524年航行进入纽约港，并将其命名为昂古莱姆。
lines.raw: Verrazano, an Italian about whom little is known, sailed into New York Harbour in 1524 and named it Angouleme. | 韦拉扎诺，一位鲜为人知的意大利人，于1524年航行进入纽约港，并将其命名为昂古莱姆。

lines.english: He described it as 'a very agreeable situation located within two small hills in the midst of which flowed a great river.'
lines.chinese: 他将其描述为“一个非常宜人的地方，坐落在两座小山之间，中间流淌着一条大河。”
lines.raw: He described it as 'a very agreeable situation located within two small hills in the midst of which flowed a great river.' | 他将其描述为“一个非常宜人的地方，坐落在两座小山之间，中间流淌着一条大河。”

lines.english: Though Verrazano is by no means considered to be a great explorer, his name will probably remain immortal,
lines.chinese: 尽管韦拉扎诺绝不被认为是一位伟大的探险家，但他的名字可能会流芳百世，
lines.raw: Though Verrazano is by no means considered to be a great explorer, his name will probably remain immortal, | 尽管韦拉扎诺绝不被认为是一位伟大的探险家，但他的名字可能会流芳百世，

lines.english: for on November 21st, 1964, the longest suspension bridge in the world was named after him.
lines.chinese: 因为在1964年11月21日，世界上最长的吊桥以他的名字命名。
lines.raw: for on November 21st, 1964, the longest suspension bridge in the world was named after him. | 因为在1964年11月21日，世界上最长的吊桥以他的名字命名。

lines.english: The Verrazano Bridge, which was designed by Othmar Ammann, joins Brooklyn to Staten Island.
lines.chinese: 韦拉扎诺大桥由奥斯马·阿曼设计，连接着布鲁克林和史泰登岛。
lines.raw: The Verrazano Bridge, which was designed by Othmar Ammann, joins Brooklyn to Staten Island. | 韦拉扎诺大桥由奥斯马·阿曼设计，连接着布鲁克林和史泰登岛。

lines.english: It has a span of 4, 260 feet.
lines.chinese: 它有一个4260英尺的跨度。
lines.raw: It has a span of 4, 260 feet. | 它有一个4260英尺的跨度。

lines.english: The bridge is so long that the shape of the earth had to be taken into account by its designer.
lines.chinese: 这座桥非常长，以至于它的设计师不得不将地球的形状考虑在内。
lines.raw: The bridge is so long that the shape of the earth had to be taken into account by its designer. | 这座桥非常长，以至于它的设计师不得不将地球的形状考虑在内。

lines.english: Two great towers support four huge cables.
lines.chinese: 两座巨大的塔架支撑着四根巨大的缆索。
lines.raw: Two great towers support four huge cables. | 两座巨大的塔架支撑着四根巨大的缆索。

lines.english: The towers are built on immense underwater platforms made of steel and concrete.
lines.chinese: 这些塔架建在由钢和混凝土制成的巨大水下平台上。
lines.raw: The towers are built on immense underwater platforms made of steel and concrete. | 这些塔架建在由钢和混凝土制成的巨大水下平台上。

lines.english: The platforms extend to a depth of over 100 feet under the sea.
lines.chinese: 这些平台延伸到海面以下100多英尺的深度。
lines.raw: The platforms extend to a depth of over 100 feet under the sea. | 这些平台延伸到海面以下100多英尺的深度。

lines.english: These alone took sixteen months to build.
lines.chinese: 仅仅这些就花了16个月来建造。
lines.raw: These alone took sixteen months to build. | 仅仅这些就花了16个月来建造。

lines.english: Above the surface of the water, the towers rise to a height of nearly 700 feet.
lines.chinese: 在水面之上，塔架上升到近700英尺的高度。
lines.raw: Above the surface of the water, the towers rise to a height of nearly 700 feet. | 在水面之上，塔架上升到近700英尺的高度。

lines.english: They support the cables from which the bridge has been suspended.
lines.chinese: 它们支撑着悬挂大桥的缆索。
lines.raw: They support the cables from which the bridge has been suspended. | 它们支撑着悬挂大桥的缆索。

lines.english: Each of the four cables contains 26, 108 lengths of wire.
lines.chinese: 四根缆索中的每一根都含有26108股钢丝。
lines.raw: Each of the four cables contains 26, 108 lengths of wire. | 四根缆索中的每一根都含有26108股钢丝。

lines.english: It has been estimated that if the bridge were packed with cars, it would still only be carrying a third of its total capacity.
lines.chinese: 据估计，即使桥上挤满了汽车，它也只承载了其总容量的三分之一。
lines.raw: It has been estimated that if the bridge were packed with cars, it would still only be carrying a third of its total capacity. | 据估计，即使桥上挤满了汽车，它也只承载了其总容量的三分之一。

lines.english: However, size and strength are not the only important things about this bridge.
lines.chinese: 然而，尺寸和强度并不是这座桥唯一重要的方面。
lines.raw: However, size and strength are not the only important things about this bridge. | 然而，尺寸和强度并不是这座桥唯一重要的方面。

lines.english: Despite its immensity, it is both simple and elegant,
lines.chinese: 尽管它如此巨大，却既简洁又优雅，
lines.raw: Despite its immensity, it is both simple and elegant, | 尽管它如此巨大，却既简洁又优雅，

lines.english: fulfilling its designer's dream to creat 'an enormous object drawn as faintly as possible'.
lines.chinese: 实现了设计师创造“一个尽可能轻描淡写地勾勒出的巨大物体”的梦想。
lines.raw: fulfilling its designer's dream to creat 'an enormous object drawn as faintly as possible'. | 实现了设计师创造“一个尽可能轻描淡写地勾勒出的巨大物体”的梦想。
